title: More than 70 million warnings sent to people seeking child abuse material
snippet: The messages highlight the illegality of what people are searching for online and direct them towards help.

title: Trump arrives in China for high-stakes meeting with Xi Jinping
snippet: The leaders of the global superpowers are expected to discuss the war in Iran, tariffs, AI and Taiwan during the two-day visit.

title: How are countries responding to hantavirus?
snippet: The UK, US and EU are asking all citizens returning home from the virus-hit MV Hondius to self-isolate for about six weeks.

title: Smart glasses are 'an invasion of privacy' - Meta's are selling better than ever
snippet: The biggest tech firms are set to sell millions of smart glasses despite growing privacy concerns.

title: Elon Musk said control of OpenAI should go to his children, Sam Altman tells jury
snippet: Sam Altman said Elon Musk tried many times for total control of OpenAI, which he's now suing.

title: Israeli strikes on cars in southern Lebanon kill 12, health ministry says
snippet: Two children are among those reported dead after seven cars were struck in separate attacks south of Beirut.

title: Is this actually what Anne Boleyn looked like?
snippet: An algorithm has suggested images of what were thought to be Anne Boleyn might not be.

title: What to know as Trump visits Xi in China
snippet: Trump has landed in Beijing for the first visit by a US leader since his own in 2017.

title: Thousands of Waymos recalled after robotaxi swept into a creek
snippet: The voluntary recall follows an incident on 20 April where an empty Waymo car entered a flooded road in San Antonio, Texas.

title: Massive Alaska megatsunami was second largest ever recorded
snippet: New research suggests glacier melt driven by climate change is increasing the risk of giant waves.

title: WhatsApp launches 'incognito' AI chat with private disappearing messages
snippet: A cyber security expert says deleting chat history could lead to a lack of accountability if things go wrong.

title: Shrinking Milka chocolate bar tricked consumers, says German court
snippet: A court in Bremen has found the manufacturer of the classic Alpine Milk chocolate bar guilty of "shrinkflation".

title: Suicide forum fined £950,000 for not blocking UK users
snippet: The regulator said the forum had not done enough to protect UK users - but critics accuse Ofcom of acting too slowly.

title: Is this the real face of Anne Boleyn?
snippet: A computer science team believes they have discovered a previously unknown sketch of King Henry VIII's second wife - but not everyone is convinced.

title: Posh sandwich has more salt than nearly five cheeseburgers
snippet: Action on Salt & Sugar said people should not be exposed to a "hidden health risk every time they buy lunch".
